title: [cofagrigus] Battle Guide
summary: A 145 Def [ghost] wall that flips the [mummy] ability onto attackers to delete huge-power, guts, and moxie on contact.
strengths: 145 Def [bold] walls nearly every physical attacker in the format
[mummy] overwrites enemy abilities on contact — a hard counter to guts and huge-power
[will-o-wisp] and [pain-split] create a unique sustain loop
weaknesses: 30 Speed means every move lands on it first
105 SpD isn't enough to live two hits from top special attackers

recommendedMoves.reason: Only reliable STAB to punish psychic and [ghost] switch-ins
Cuts physical attacker damage in half and enables long-term sustain
Turns a 58 HP stat into a relative-HP recovery tool
Erases every stat boost from setup sweepers in one click

recommendedItems.reason: Baseline passive recovery for long-term cycles
50 percent HP one-shot heal for the initial entry turn
Clears taunt so the utility plan survives bait leads

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[cofagrigus] is built around one stat and one ability — base 145 Def and [mummy]. The ability replaces an attacker's ability with [mummy] the moment they make contact, which cleanly disables some of the most dangerous offensive abilities in the format (huge-power, guts, moxie, protean). That single-click interaction matters more than most of [cofagrigus]'s moves.

## Strengths
With a [bold] nature and full defensive investment, [cofagrigus] walls the entire physical tier. Mummy turns any attacker that clicks a contact move into a neutered shell — Diggersby goes from huge-power to [mummy], Conkeldurr loses iron-fist, Krookodile loses moxie. [will-o-wisp] cuts physical damage by half on top of that, so most physical sweepers effectively waste a turn clicking into [cofagrigus]. [pain-split] is unique as a wall tool — with only 58 HP base, [cofagrigus] almost always gains HP from this while also draining the opposing attacker.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[ghost] and [dark] are 2x weaknesses and both are common — a [dark]-type pivot's knock-off or [dark]-pulse forces [cofagrigus] out immediately. 105 SpD holds up okay but isn't enough to stand in front of a top-tier special attacker. Speed 30 means every move lands first, so a taunt before [will-o-wisp] shuts the whole plan down, which is why [mental-herb] matters. [pain-split] also backfires when the opponent has less HP than [cofagrigus], so it isn't a blind recovery click.

## Recommended Moveset
[will-o-wisp] is the locked utility — half of [cofagrigus]'s role is burning physical attackers on entry. [shadow-ball] is the only real attack; off 95 SpA it's not huge but it keeps psychic and [ghost] attackers from sitting for free. [pain-split] substitutes for recover and also acts as a second form of chip on the opponent. [haze] is the fourth slot to erase setup sweeper boosts — a single turn of [haze] saves the game against dragon-dance. hex is a viable alternative to [shadow-ball] when the build runs [will-o-wisp] every game, doubling damage on burned targets.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default — [cofagrigus] needs every sustain source it can stack. [sitrus-berry] is the alternative for builds that want a single big heal on entry rather than chip recovery. [mental-herb] is a sideways pick into teams that lead with taunt. The ability is locked as [mummy] and that's precisely the point of the Pokémon. Tera [fairy] is the strongest defensive Tera, flipping both the [ghost] and [dark] weaknesses into resistances and immunities while gaining a Fighting resistance. Tera [dark] is a more aggressive pivot, removing the psychic threat space entirely.

## Teammates & Synergy
[cofagrigus] covers the physical half of the defensive core — pair it with a dedicated special wall (Blissey, Chansey, or a calm-mind [fairy]). A [dark]-resist partner (Fighting or [fairy]) answers the matchup where [cofagrigus] cannot stay in. A hazard setter amplifies [pain-split] damage by reducing opponent HP on every switch. [cofagrigus] fits naturally into a trick-room team thanks to its base 30 Speed, where it can click moves first for a change.

## Play Patterns
Bring [cofagrigus] in specifically on physical setup sweepers — the turn a swords-dance or dragon-dance is telegraphed is the right entry. Click [will-o-wisp] first, then [haze] if a boost landed, then [shadow-ball] or [pain-split] based on opponent HP. Never stay in against [dark]-pulse or [shadow-ball] attackers. Save Tera for the matchup where [cofagrigus] is the only answer to a [dark] or [ghost] breaker, because losing it early usually means losing the game state entirely.
